name: The Fairmont-Morgantown Housing Authority
description_short: Public housing authority providing Section 8 vouchers, public housing rental assistance, and HUD-approved housing counseling to low-income residents in Fairmont-Morgantown, WV.
description_long:
The Fairmont-Morgantown Housing Authority (FMHA) is a government housing agency serving Marion and Monongalia counties in West Virginia. Established under HUD oversight, it administers federal rental assistance programs designed to help low-to-moderate-income families access affordable housing. The agency operates from its principal office at 103 12th Street in Fairmont.

FMHA offers three primary service lines: Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers (rental subsidies for private landlords), Public Housing (directly-operated rental units including Gaston Avenue Apartments, Miller School Apartments, and Seneca Village Apartments), and a HomeOwnership Center providing HUD-approved housing counseling, mortgage financing guidance, and homebuyer education. The agency also manages project-based vouchers tied to specific properties and provides fair housing education and complaint resolution.

As a government entity rather than a for-profit lender, FMHA distinguishes itself through zero-cost housing counseling, income-based eligibility (published in their income guidelines), transparent fee schedules, and compliance with Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) protections. Their services are funded by HUD and serve exclusively low-income households, with no credit-based lending or profit motive.

The agency faced operational disruptions from recent flooding (as of June 2025), temporarily closing phone lines and closing their Section 8 waiting list. This reflects vulnerability to local infrastructure issues and typical challenges of underfunded public agencies. Waiting lists for public housing can be extended, and eligibility is strictly income-based, limiting accessibility for moderate-income households.
pros: Free HUD-approved housing counseling through HomeOwnership Center (no fees)
Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers provide rental subsidies for private market housing
Direct public housing with affordable rents tied to income levels
VAWA protections and fair housing enforcement on all programs
Income guidelines and fee schedule published transparently on website
Project-based vouchers available at specific apartment communities
Smoke-free policy and written lease protections for tenants
Public grievance procedures for housing disputes
cons: Section 8 waitlist currently closed with no reopening date announced (as of June 2025)
Public Housing one-bedroom waiting list closed; extended wait times typical for public housing
Eligibility strictly income-based; does not serve moderate or higher-income households
Recent flooding disrupted phone service; relies on email for contact during emergencies
Limited geographic service area (Marion and Monongalia counties only)
best_for: Low-income families seeking affordable rental housing in Marion/Monongalia counties
First-time homebuyers wanting free HUD-approved counseling and mortgage guidance
Renters needing fair housing advocacy or tenant rights assistance
Households experiencing housing instability after disaster or emergency (flood assistance provided)
diagnosis: FMHA is best for low-income households in Fairmont-Morgantown seeking subsidized rental housing or free housing counseling. The main caveat is that eligibility is strictly income-based and current waitlists are closed, making access delayed or unavailable depending on program and timing.
services: Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher administration and rental subsidies
Public Housing rental assistance with income-based affordability
Fair housing complaint investigation and enforcement
Project-based vouchers (Gaston Avenue, Miller School, Seneca Village Apartments)
VAWA certification and occupancy rights notification
Public housing lease execution and grievance procedures
EIV (Enterprise Income Verification) fraud prevention education
Orientation briefings for new voucher holders
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
